title: Noah Lucas homepage v019 clear liquid glass and theme fade endpoints

source.localSource: v018 release corrected per Noah feedback

changeSummary: Adds a Liquid glass handling selector with Clear, Ultrathin, Thin, Regular, and Thick modes.
Defaults the page to Clear and syncs the selected mode across every data-hgl-mode surface.
Changes the scroll scene treatment so the image layer fades to opacity zero over the theme endpoint: white in light mode and black in dark mode.
Disables the body pseudo-element overlay for scene fading so the background image is not tinted or covered by an overlay.
Keeps the v018 Serve Robotics static image and white media panel behavior intact.

validation.method: Playwright via system Google Chrome against local preview URL
validation.flowUnderTest: v019 homepage preview -> switch theme/glass and scroll into lower sections -> image fades to white/black endpoint and selected glass state applies.
checks: Default glass is Clear and the selector shows Clear at load.
Changing the selector to Thick updates html[data-glass], every data-hgl-mode surface, and the computed pane blur/fill.
Returning to Clear restores every data-hgl-mode surface and the computed clear pane blur/fill.
At lower scroll, the scene stage opacity is 0.000 and the light endpoint computes to rgb(255, 255, 255).
At lower scroll after switching to dark theme, the scene stage opacity is 0.000 and the dark endpoint computes to rgb(0, 0, 0).
body::after is disabled, and the hero image renders with filter none, normal blend mode, and no mask.
No console messages or page errors were recorded; remote video request aborts are non-blocking in the local headless teardown.

supersedesReason: v019 fixes glass-mode inconsistency and scroll-scene theme endpoints while preserving the v018 Serve media correction.
